description: VelesDB Performance Baseline v1.7.3 — re-baselined 2026-05-13 post PR #638 (Collection::search refactor)

environment.runner: ubuntu-latest (GitHub Actions, 2-core) — post PR #638 delta-merge overhead

pq_recall/pq_recall_m8_k256_rescore.notes: PQ m=8 k=256, rescore=4x, 5K/128d uniform random dataset

pq_recall/full_precision_baseline.notes: Full precision HNSW reference, 5K/128d uniform random dataset

pq_recall/pq_recall_m8_k256_no_rescore.notes: No rescore - raw HNSW with PQ distances

pq_recall/opq_recall_m8_k256_rescore.notes: OPQ with PCA rotation, 5K/128d uniform random dataset

pq_recall/pq_recall_m8_k256_oversampling.notes: PQ with 8x oversampling, 5K/128d uniform random dataset

notes: v1.7.2 (2026-03-24): Re-baselined for ubuntu-latest GitHub Actions runners (2-core AMD)
smoke_insert avg 9.3s (7.86/9.87/9.47/8.80/10.42 across 5 CI runs) — high variance (IO-bound), threshold=25%

v1.7.3 (2026-05-13): Re-baselined post PR #638 (merged 2026-04-23) which refactored Collection::search() into collection/search/vector.rs, adding delta-merge and component-score tagging overhead (~25 % slowdown on 10K/128D)
smoke_search: v1.7.2 CI baseline was 338µs (pre-#638); post-#638 CI runs showed 390-420µs. New baseline=420µs, threshold=25% accommodates CI runner variance.
smoke_hybrid: similarly adjusted to 410µs with 25% threshold to match smoke_insert policy.
Issue #377 tracks the separate target of <30µs for 10K/768D (index-only) — not this end-to-end regression guard.
pq_recall entries hardened in Phase 11 — 6 variants with explicit m=8 k=256 training via VelesQL
PQ recall thresholds restored to 0.92 (PQ-07 contract) with uniform random data
